sn.s: En Bekannte fragt mi scho wieder um Gäld.
sn.e: An acquaintance asks me for money again.

sn.s: Es isch s'dritt Mal de Monet.
sn.e: It's the third time this month.

sn.s: Ich wott hälfe, aber au e Gränze setze.
sn.e: I want to help, but also set a boundary.

sn.s: "Lueg, ich schätze di, aber das Mal chan ich nöd."
sn.e: "Look, I appreciate you, but this time I can't."

sn.s: "Leider langt's mer de Monet sälber nöd."
sn.e: "Unfortunately I don't have enough myself this month."
I say it calmly, but firmly.

sn.s: "Isch guet. Merci, dass d'ehrlich bisch," seit er.
sn.e: "It's okay. Thanks for being honest," he says.

sn.s: "Klar. Bi öppisem anderem hilf ich gärn."
sn.e: "Of course. I'll gladly help with something else."

sn.s: Nei säge isch au e Form vo Respekt.
sn.e: Saying no is also a form of respect.

sn.s: Respekt für sich sälber und für de ander.
sn.e: Respect for yourself and for the other person.

sn.s: Ehrlich und fründlich zäme sii isch möglich.
sn.e: Being honest and kind at once is possible.

sn.s: E Gränze setze uf Schwiizerdütsch: en chliine Sieg.
sn.e: Setting a boundary in Swiss German: a small victory.
